hero.cexUploadLink: Upload your trade history for the same diagnosis →

behavior.sectionTitle: Your trading history hides habits you won't admit
behavior.sectionSubtitle: Portfolio trackers show your holdings. Only MiaoFi reads your trading history like a behavioral psychologist.

fomo.label: FOMO Chasing
fomo.finding: You didn't buy SOL when it dropped from $295 to $120. You chased it at $180 after a 50% bounce. Now it's $80. You're down 56%.
fomo.prescription: All 3 of your buys came after 30%+ price rebounds. If you had DCA'd at $120 (weekly buys), your cost basis would be $105, not $180. Set a rule: only add positions below the 30-day moving average.
fomo.rule: 📋 Rule: only add positions below the 30-day MA

disposition.label: Won't Take Profit, Won't Cut Losses
disposition.finding: Your ETH went from the $4,900 peak to $2,000 with zero sells in between. $19,000 in unrealized gains turned into $10,000 in unrealized losses. This isn't the first time — 3 tokens went through the full gain-to-loss cycle in the past 12 months.
disposition.prescription: If you'd sold half your ETH at $4,000 (-18% from peak), you'd have locked in $5,000 profit. The other half at today's price loses $5,000. Net: $0 (breakeven) vs your current -$10,000.
disposition.rule: 📋 Rule: auto-trim 1/3 of any position up 50%+

panic.label: Panic Selling
panic.finding: On Feb 3, 2026, ETH dropped 8% in a single day. You sold your entire position near the bottom. Price rebounded 11% within 3 days. You sold the floor. This happened twice in the past 6 months.
panic.prescription: Cost of panic selling: you'd have $2,400 more if you'd held. If you're worried about downside, the right move is staged selling, not one-click liquidation. Set a rule: max 25% of position per sell, wait 24 hours before the next move.
panic.rule: 📋 Rule: max 25% per sell, wait 24 hours

behavior.cta: 🔍 Your trading habits are eating your profits. Find out in 30 seconds →

realDiagnoses.title: Real Wallets. Real Diagnoses.
realDiagnoses.subtitle: These are MiaoFi's actual analyses of on-chain public wallets. Any wallet can be diagnosed.
realDiagnoses.vitalikDiagnosis: Sold 19K ETH after a 37% drawdown. If he'd trimmed at $4,000, the same cash need would've required selling only 9,700 ETH — half the coins for the same money
realDiagnoses.vitalikFootnote: Based on public on-chain data · Verifiable via Arkham Intelligence
realDiagnoses.degenDiagnosis: Bought 5 meme coins in one week. 3 went to zero. The other 2 are down 60%. It's not bad luck — there's zero risk management logic
realDiagnoses.degenFootnote: Based on public on-chain data · Verifiable via Solscan
realDiagnoses.sleeperTag: Diamond Hands Sleeper · Maybe it's you
realDiagnoses.sleeperDiagnosis: $28K USDC sitting in wallet for 4 months. That's $1,120 in yield gifted to the exchange. A few clicks would put it to work in DeFi

security.title: Security & Trust
security.subtitle: Your security is our top priority
security.item1Title: Read-only. We never touch your coins
security.item1Desc: Pasting an address ≠ authorizing transactions. We only read public on-chain data, just like checking an address on a blockchain explorer
security.item2Desc: We don't store keys, sign transactions, or touch assets. Your coins stay exactly where they are
security.item3Desc: Multiple top AI models analyze simultaneously, then human investment experts review. Machines find patterns, humans verify judgment — reducing single-source bias and hallucination
security.item4Desc: All analysis is based on public blockchain records. No data breach risk because the data is public by nature

bottomCta.subtitle: Review your trading behavior. Find the patterns that keep costing you money. 30 seconds. Free.
bottomCta.trustLine: Free · Read-only · Never touches assets · Results in 30 seconds

footer.tagline: AI + Investment Experts — Your Crypto Investment Copilot

cexUpload.pageSubtitle: No on-chain wallet? No problem. Upload your trade history and AI will diagnose your trading behavior
cexUpload.uploadDesc: Drag & drop your file here, or click to browse
cexUpload.howToExport: How to export trade history from Binance?
cexUpload.howToStep1: Log in to Binance web, go to "Trade" → "Spot"
cexUpload.howToStep2: Click "Trade History" in the top right, or go to "Orders" → "Spot Orders" → "Trade History"
cexUpload.howToStep3: Select "Spot" type, set the time range you want to analyze (recommend at least 3 months)
cexUpload.howToStep4: Click "Export" button, choose Excel or CSV format
cexUpload.howToStep5: Download and come back here to upload
cexUpload.warningPartialParse: Some records had format issues and were skipped
cexUpload.analyzing: AI is analyzing your trading behavior...
cexUpload.analyzingDesc: Multiple AI models are cross-validating your trading patterns

cexResults.pageSubtitle: AI + investment expert cross-diagnosis based on your Binance trade history
cexResults.behaviorSubtitle: AI identified these behavioral patterns from your trading history
cexResults.pnlSubtitle: Historical cost & avg price by asset (not real-time holdings)

traderType.overtraderDesc: You trade too frequently — fees and poor timing are silently eating your gains.
traderType.impulseChaserDesc: You tend to buy into hype — entries near local tops are eroding your returns.

benchmark.attribution: FOMO entries, revenge trades, and missing stop-losses account for 82% of underperformance vs BTC hold strategy.

worstTrades.wt1Comment: Held through 35% drawdown with no stop-loss. A -10% stop would have saved $2,400.
worstTrades.wt2Comment: Bought after 18% pump. Classic FOMO entry near the local top.
worstTrades.wt3Comment: Entered after news hype. Still holding at a loss — unrealized.

overtrading.label: Overtrading
overtrading.summary: 847 trades in 9 months, avg 3.1/day. Frequent trading didn't improve returns — it cost you $2,340 in fees. Every trade is paying the exchange.
overtrading.recommendation: Consolidate to 5-8 trades per month. Each unnecessary trade erodes returns through fees and poor timing. Set a weekly trade limit and stick to it.
overtrading.action1Desc: Cap at 2 trades per week to reduce noise
overtrading.action2Desc: See how much fees ate into your returns
overtrading.action3Desc: Add a 6h buffer between consecutive trades

fomo.label: FOMO Chasing
fomo.summary: 4 out of 5 BTC and ETH buys came after 15%+ price rebounds. Your avg entry is 22% above the 30-day average.
fomo.recommendation: Set limit orders at your target price before hype cycles. Wait 4+ hours after a surge before evaluating entry. Historical data shows 78% of FOMO entries end in losses within 2 weeks.
fomo.action1Desc: Block buy orders for 4h after 15%+ surges
fomo.action2Desc: Get notified at your target price, not at the top
fomo.action3Desc: See how past surges played out before buying

revenge.label: Revenge Trading
revenge.summary: 6 times you opened new trades within 2 hours of a big loss. 5 of those also lost money. Trying to win it back only dug the hole deeper.
revenge.recommendation: After a loss exceeding 5%, enforce a mandatory 24-hour cooling period before your next trade. Your win rate drops to 17% on revenge trades vs 42% normal.
revenge.action1Desc: Block trading for 24h after any loss > 5%
revenge.action2Desc: Stop trading if daily loss exceeds $500
revenge.action3Desc: See the pattern of losses after losses

noStopLoss.label: No Stop-Loss Habit
noStopLoss.summary: Zero stop-losses set in 9 months. Max single loss: -$3,200 (SOL). A -10% stop-loss would have capped it at $800.
noStopLoss.recommendation: Set a -10% stop-loss on every position. This single habit would have saved you $2,400 on your worst trade alone.
noStopLoss.action1Desc: Sell automatically at a planned level
noStopLoss.action2Desc: Lock in profits while limiting downside
noStopLoss.action3Desc: See how stop-losses would have changed your P&L

ethConcentration.title: 60% Portfolio Concentrated in ETH
ethConcentration.body: Your ETH position ($28,500) represents 60% of your total trading volume. Combined with BTC ($42,800), you have 90% exposure to just 2 assets. If ETH drops 30%, you lose ~$6,400.
ethConcentration.action1Desc: Lock in profits and reduce volatility
ethConcentration.action3Desc: Get warned when any asset exceeds 40%
ethConcentration.detail: ETH represents a dangerous concentration risk. With 60% of trading volume in a single asset, a market correction could wipe out a significant portion of your portfolio. Historical data shows concentrated portfolios underperform diversified ones by 4-7% annually on a risk-adjusted basis.

feeBleeding.title: Trading Fees Are Eating Your Profits
feeBleeding.body: You've paid $2,340 in trading fees over 9 months — that's $260/month. At your current trading frequency (3.1 trades/day), you need each trade to profit >0.3% just to break even on fees.
feeBleeding.action1Desc: Maker fees are 50-80% cheaper than taker fees
feeBleeding.action2Desc: Fewer trades = fewer fees = better returns
feeBleeding.action3Desc: Your volume qualifies for lower fee tiers
feeBleeding.detail: At 847 trades over 9 months, your average fee per trade is $2.76. If you reduced to 200 trades (still plenty for active trading), you'd save ~$1,787 in fees alone. Combined with better entry timing from less impulsive trading, the total improvement could be $3,000-5,000.

dogeProfit.title: DOGE Is Your Only Winner — Consider Why
dogeProfit.body: DOGE is the only asset where you made money (+$1,600). Interestingly, you traded DOGE less frequently and held longer. This pattern suggests your best returns come from patience, not activity.
dogeProfit.action1Desc: Force yourself to hold winners longer
dogeProfit.action2Desc: Trade your main assets like you trade DOGE
dogeProfit.detail: Your DOGE trades had an average hold time of 12.3 days vs 2.1 days for BTC/ETH. The longer hold time allowed you to capture larger moves instead of getting shaken out by daily volatility. This is a clear signal that reducing frequency improves your returns.

portfolio.title: Portfolio Analysis
portfolio.summaryDesc: Emotional trading with no stop-losses and heavy concentration in 2 assets
